title: Translate the public explainer (HIGH) into priority languages
context: BACKLOG: Only after M4 ships (the oncologist + patient-advocate reviewed public explainer). Translating the explainer into languages spoken by affected communities (e.g. Spanish, Simplified Chinese, Vietnamese, Tagalog — chosen based on the partner's community) extends reach and equity. HIGH risk: translated patient-facing health education content requires the same framing and clinical-accuracy sign-off as the English original, in each target language, with a native-speaker/community-representative review. Per-language framing + Onc+Adv review required.
objective: Translate the reviewed public explainer (disparities-analysis-explainer-014) into priority languages chosen based on the delivery partner's community (e.g. Spanish, Simplified Chinese, Vietnamese, Tagalog). Each translation must: be reviewed by a native-speaker/community representative familiar with health-equity framing in that language; carry the same persistent 'not medical advice' label (translated appropriately); maintain non-stigmatizing, person-first framing in the target language; and have oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off before publication. The 'not medical advice' caveat must not be diluted or omitted in translation.
acceptanceCriteria: Target languages are selected based on the delivery partner's community demographics and confirmed with the partner/steward before translation begins
Each translated explainer carries the persistent 'not medical advice — consult your care team' label (translated appropriately and prominently, not only in a footnote) in the target language
Each translation is reviewed by a native speaker familiar with health-equity and health-literacy framing in the target language and cultural context; review recorded
Non-stigmatizing, person-first, community-centered framing is maintained in each target language; a framing reviewer with cultural competency in the target language confirms this
Oncologist sign-off recorded for each target-language version (blocking): confirms clinical accuracy and 'not medical advice' framing are upheld in translation
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded for each target-language version (blocking): confirms community appropriateness and language accessibility

Each translated version is linked to the source English version and the translation date is recorded; the translation is marked as requiring re-verification on any material update to the English source
output: Reviewed, oncologist + patient-advocate approved translations of the public explainer into priority languages, each with a native-speaker/community framing review, persistent 'not medical advice' label, and per-language sign-off recorded.
